PlayerProfile.title: PlayerProfile
PlayerProfile.description: Represents a player's account profile in the Axiom Frontier application, storing core identity, game progression, and resource information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PlayerProfile entity.

email.description: The player's email address, used for contact and account recovery (stored without authentication data).

creationDate.description: Timestamp when the player's profile was created.

lastLoginDate.description: Timestamp of the player's last login.

axiomCoins.description: The current amount of Axiom Coins (premium currency) owned by the player.

inGameCurrency.description: The current amount of in-game standard currency owned by the player.

agentIds.description: References to Agents owned by this player. (Relationship: PlayerProfile 1:N Agent)

inventoryItemIds.description: References to items directly held in the player's inventory. (Relationship: PlayerProfile N:N Item, typically via a junction entity to handle quantity/durability for instance-specific items)

Agent.title: Agent
Agent.description: Represents an autonomous in-game agent operating on behalf of a player, performing actions like gathering, combat, or trading.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Agent entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the PlayerProfile who owns this agent. (Relationship: PlayerProfile 1:N Agent)

type.description: The specialized role or type of the agent (e.g., 'Gatherer', 'Combatant', 'Trader', 'Explorer').

currentLocation.description: The current in-game location or coordinates of the agent.

health.description: The current health points of the agent.

energy.description: The current energy points of the agent, used for actions.

experience.description: The accumulated experience points of the agent.

status.description: The current operational status of the agent (e.g., 'idle', 'gathering', 'fighting', 'travelling').

inventoryItemIds.description: References to items currently held in the agent's inventory. (Relationship: Agent N:N Item, typically via a junction entity to handle quantity/durability)

Item.title: Item
Item.description: Defines a type of item available within the game world, including resources, equipment, and consumables.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Item entity.

name.description: The display name of the item.

description.description: A detailed description of the item and its uses.

baseValue.description: The base in-game currency value of the item.

weight.description: The weight of a single unit of the item, impacting inventory capacity.

stackable.description: Indicates if multiple units of this item can be stacked in a single inventory slot.

maxStackSize.description: The maximum number of units that can be stacked together, if stackable.

gameAssetId.description: Reference to the GameAsset representing this item's visual model or icon. (Relationship: GameAsset 1:N Item)

WorldState.title: WorldState
WorldState.description: Stores global, evolving parameters and aggregate data representing the current state of the game world.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorldState entity (expected to be a singleton, e.g., 'global_state').

civilizationIndex.description: A numerical index representing the overall development and influence of civilizations in the world.

currentInflationRate.description: The current global inflation rate affecting in-game economy.

lastHeartbeat.description: Timestamp of the last successful 'world heartbeat' cron job execution.

gamePhase.description: The current overarching phase of the game world (e.g., 'Discovery', 'Expansion', 'Conflict', 'Post-Singularity').

activeQuestIds.description: References to quests that are currently active and globally available. (Relationship: WorldState N:N Quest)

gameSettingIds.description: References to global game settings applied to this world state. (Relationship: WorldState N:N GameSetting)

Quest.title: Quest
Quest.description: Represents a dynamic mission or objective within the game, potentially generated by AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quest entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the quest objective and background.

status.description: The current status of the quest (e.g., 'Available', 'Active', 'Completed', 'Failed').

assignedToPlayerId.description: Reference to the PlayerProfile to whom this quest is currently assigned. (Relationship: PlayerProfile 1:N Quest)

npcIssuerId.description: Reference to the NPC who issued or is associated with this quest. (Relationship: NPC 1:N Quest)

rewardItemIds.description: References to items awarded upon completion of this quest. (Relationship: Quest N:N Item)

generatedByAI.description: Indicates if this quest was dynamically generated by the AI.

generationTimestamp.description: Timestamp when this quest was generated, if applicable.

objectiveDetails.description: A structured string (e.g., JSON string) detailing the specific objectives (e.g., 'kill X enemies', 'fetch Y item', 'reach Z location').

NPC.title: NPC
NPC.description: Represents a Non-Player Character in the game world, potentially with AI-generated personalities and dialogue.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NPC entity.

personalityDescription.description: A description of the NPC's personality, dynamically generated by AI.

currentLocation.description: The current in-game location or coordinates of the NPC.

dialogueIds.description: References to predefined or AI-generated dialogue options associated with this NPC. (Relationship: NPC N:N Dialogue - assuming a Dialogue entity if complex dialogue management is needed)

questOfferIds.description: References to quests that this NPC can offer or is involved with. (Relationship: NPC N:N Quest)

GameAsset.title: GameAsset
GameAsset.description: Manages metadata for in-game assets and textures stored in Firebase Storage, for dynamic loading in Godot.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameAsset entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the asset.

storagePath.description: The URI or path to the asset file within Firebase Storage.

version.description: The version identifier of the asset, for update management.

metadata.description: Additional metadata about the asset, stored as a JSON string (e.g., '{"resolution": "1024x1024", "author": "Axiom Studios"}').

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Records a purchase order made by a player through the Axiom Storefront.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the PlayerProfile who placed the order. (Relationship: PlayerProfile 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: Timestamp when the order was placed.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Completed', 'Failed', 'Refunded').

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order.

currencyType.description: The type of currency used for the transaction (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'AxiomCoins').

transactionId.description: Reference to the associated Transaction entity, if paid via an external provider. (Relationship: Order 1:1 Transaction)

orderItemIds.description: References to the OrderItem entities included in this order. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single line item within an Order, detailing the specific item purchased and its quantity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order to which this item belongs. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

itemId.description: Reference to the Item definition being purchased. (Relationship: Item 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The number of units of the item purchased.

pricePerUnit.description: The price of a single unit of the item at the time of purchase.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records details of external payment transactions (e.g., PayPal) associated with orders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order associated with this transaction. (Relationship: Order 1:1 Transaction)

paymentProvider.description: The name of the external payment provider (e.g., 'PayPal').

providerTransactionId.description: The unique transaction ID provided by the external payment gateway.

amount.description: The monetary amount of the transaction.

currency.description: The currency code of the transaction (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

GameSetting.title: GameSetting
GameSetting.description: Stores configurable parameters for game administration, affecting global game logic and behavior.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameSetting entity.

settingKey.description: The unique key identifying this game setting (e.g., 'initial_player_currency', 'loot_drop_rate').

settingValue.description: The value of the game setting, stored as a string (can be parsed to number, boolean, or JSON as needed).

description.description: A description of what this setting controls.

lastModifiedBy.description: Identifier of the administrator or system that last modified this setting.

lastModifiedDate.description: Timestamp of the last modification to this setting.

definition.description: Stores individual player profiles. This is a top-level collection where each document ID corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID, enabling direct, secure, and performant access control based on `request.auth.uid`. Authorization is fully independent, as ownership is implicit in the path itself.

Stores in-game agents owned by a specific player. This subcollection uses path-based ownership for authorization independence, where the parent `playerId` dictates access. The `Agent` document also includes its `playerId` field, further simplifying security rules like `request.auth.uid == playerId` without `get()` calls. Supports QAPs by allowing users to list only their own agents.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the PlayerProfile owning this agent, directly mapping to Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: agentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific agent.

definition.description: Stores quests specifically assigned to and managed by a player. This subcollection uses path-based ownership for authorization independence, where the parent `playerId` dictates access. This segregation from `globalQuests` ensures different security postures for assigned vs. globally available quests. Supports QAPs by allowing users to list only their own assigned quests.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the PlayerProfile to whom this quest is assigned, directly mapping to Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: questId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific quest.

definition.description: Records purchase orders made by a specific player. This subcollection leverages path-based ownership (`playerId`) for authorization independence, making `request.auth.uid == playerId` a direct rule. This ensures players can only access their own orders. Supports QAPs by allowing users to list only their own orders.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the PlayerProfile who placed this order, directly mapping to Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific order.

definition.description: Stores individual items within a player's order. Nested under `Order` to maintain clear hierarchy and inherit `playerId` for authorization independence. Rules can use the `playerId` wildcard from the parent path. Supports QAPs as access is tied to the parent order's ownership.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the PlayerProfile associated with the parent order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific item within the order.

definition.description: Records external payment transactions associated with a player's order. Nested under `Order` to maintain clear hierarchy and inherit `playerId` for authorization independence. Rules can use the `playerId` wildcard from the parent path. Access is restricted to the owning player (read-only after creation by server) ensuring data integrity. Supports QAPs as access is tied to the parent order's ownership.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the PlayerProfile associated with the parent order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific transaction.

definition.description: A global catalog of all item definitions in the game. These documents are read-only for all players (QAP compliant, as they are public data) and writable only by authorized administrators or server processes. Authorization is independent as no player-specific data affects access to these definitions.

Stores the global, singleton game world state (expected to be a single document with ID e.g., 'global_state'). This collection is globally readable for all players and game clients (QAP compliant), but writable only by the 'world heartbeat' Cloud Function or administrators. Authorization is independent as access is not tied to any specific user data.

Stores quests that are globally available for players to discover and accept. This collection is read-only for all players (QAP compliant) and writable by server (AI generation) or administrators. This segregation from player-assigned quests ensures distinct security postures and authorization independence.

A global collection of Non-Player Characters. These documents are globally readable for all players (QAP compliant) and writable by server (AI generation) or administrators. Authorization is independent as access is not tied to player-specific data.

Manages metadata for static game assets. This collection is globally readable for all game clients to dynamically load assets (QAP compliant) and writable only by administrators or deployment processes. Authorization is independent as access is not tied to player-specific data.

Stores global game configuration parameters. These settings are generally readable by all game clients (QAP compliant) but only writable by authorized administrators or server processes, ensuring game integrity. Authorization is independent as access is not tied to player-specific data.

A dedicated collection for Database Access Control (DBAC) to identify administrators. The *existence* of a document at this path, where `adminId` matches `request.auth.uid`, grants administrative privileges. This design prioritizes 'existence over content' for simple and atomic role checks. While it reuses the 'PlayerProfile' schema reference for compliance, the document content can be minimal (e.g., just `{ 'role': 'admin' }`). Authorization is independent as it only relies on `request.auth.uid` matching the document ID.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of a player who has been granted administrative privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for Axiom Frontier is built upon core design principles to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. A primary focus is placed on **Authorization Independence** and **Structural Segregation** to simplify security rules and enhance performance.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
All player-specific data, such as `PlayerProfile` itself, `Agent`s, `Order`s, `OrderItem`s, `Transaction`s, and assigned `Quest`s, are organized under the `/playerProfiles/{playerId}/` root. For instance, `agents` are stored at `/playerProfiles/{playerId}/agents/{agentId}`. This hierarchical path intrinsically denormalizes the `playerId` (which maps directly to `request.auth.uid`) into the document's full path. This design is **critical** because it allows Firestore security rules to evaluate authorization solely by checking `request.auth.uid == playerId` (where `playerId` is the path wildcard) *without* needing to perform a `get()` operation on a parent `PlayerProfile` document. This eliminates transactional dependencies on parent documents, prevents potential deadlocks, and simplifies rule logic, making it robust and easy to debug. Each subcollection document (e.g., Agent, Order) also explicitly contains a `playerId` field for enhanced queryability and data consistency, serving as redundant denormalization.

**Structural Segregation for Homogeneous Security Posture:**
Data is segregated into distinct collections based on its security requirements. For example:
*   **Player-Owned Data:** Collections like `/playerProfiles/{playerId}`, `/playerProfiles/{playerId}/agents`, `/playerProfiles/{playerId}/orders`, and `/playerProfiles/{playerId}/quests` (for assigned quests) are strictly owned by and accessible only to the authenticated player.
*   **Global Public Data:** Collections such as `/items`, `/npcs`, `/gameAssets`, `/worldState`, `/globalQuests`, and `/gameSettings` are designed to be globally readable by all game clients. This separation ensures that rules for these collections are simple ('allow read: if true;') and don't interfere with player-specific logic.
*   **Admin Data:** A dedicated collection `/roles_admin/{adminId}` is used for Database Access Control (DBAC), where the *existence* of a document at a specific `adminId` (matching `request.auth.uid`) grants administrative privileges. This adheres to the 'existence over content' principle, providing a clear and efficient role check.

**QAPs (Queries are not Filters) Enforcement:**
*   For player-owned data (e.g., `agents`, `orders`), the path-based ownership (`/playerProfiles/{playerId}/...`) inherently enforces Query-time Authorization Predicates (QAPs). A player can only `list` documents within their `/playerProfiles/{request.auth.uid}/` subcollections, meaning the rules naturally filter results to only what the user is authorized to see. This makes client-side queries safe and efficient without needing additional server-side filtering logic.
*   For global collections (`/items`, `/npcs`, etc.), the security posture is 'read-all'. Therefore, a `list` operation on these collections is intended to return all documents (within query limits), which aligns with their public nature and is QAP compliant as there are no hidden documents based on user permissions.

**Other Principles:**
*   **DBAC:** Admin roles are managed via the `/roles_admin/{adminId}` collection, ensuring authorization relies solely on `request.auth.uid`.
*   **Clarity of Intent:** Wildcards like `{playerId}`, `{agentId}`, `{orderId}` are descriptive and clearly indicate the data's ownership and scope. The use of a `status` field in entities like `Quest` and `Order` explicitly models state transitions.
*   **Predictable Schema:** While not explicitly shown in the paths, the entities adhere to predictable schemas, avoiding dynamic keys.

This structure prioritizes a clear and unambiguous authorization model, making security rule implementation straightforward, robust, and performant.
